pattern: Every teleport chain only ever visits indices that are k apart, so the magicians split into k independent chains; walking each chain from its tail backward turns 'sum everything from here to the end' into an O(1) update per step.
hints: A starting index i only ever visits i, i+k, i+2k, ... -- indices congruent to i modulo k never interact with any other residue class.
For a fixed chain, the total energy collected starting at position i is just energy[i] plus whatever starting at i+k would collect, so process each chain from its last index back to its first.
Let suffix[i] = energy[i] + (suffix[i + k] if i + k is still in range else 0), computed for every index from n-1 down to 0 regardless of chain, then the answer is the maximum suffix[i] over all i.

edge_inputs.description: two magicians, k equals length minus one
starting anywhere but the last index is worse than starting at the last index
all zeros, every start ties at zero
k larger than half the array so most chains have a single stop

stress.description: random energy values with random valid k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100000))
    energy = [rng.randint(-1000, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n - 1)
    return [energy, k]
stress.note: n scales up to the real constraint
